headline: The case board learned not to pre-file every possible couple.
summary: Test alpha. Moves untouched relationships into a sparse relationship graph, adds John Pork and Cassia-Six to the roster, and starts fresh from older alpha saves because the pair storage contract changed.

sections.title: What's new
items: Fresh saves no longer store every possible pair. Cupid now projects untouched relationships when the desk needs a recommendation, then files an edge when a pair earns real history.
John Pork and Cassia-Six joined the roster with portraits, avatars, requests, voice fingerprints, bubble styles, and chemistry notes.
Live dates now track each member's private date state separately, so one person can warm while the other files a silent complaint with their own nervous system.

sections.title: Save reset
items: v0.3.3 and older saves are intentionally incompatible with v0.3.4. The current build starts a fresh save and preserves the previous local save as a backup when possible.
The reset is for the relationship graph change: untouched pairs are no longer stored as dense pair records.

sections.title: Fixes and polish
items: Cupid coaching notes now reach performers as private coaching instead of something to answer aloud at the table.
Performer lines keep the short-message runtime cap while the audit still reports raw model output when a model gets wordy.
Budget review no longer treats a fresh sparse graph as if every pair relationship had failed at zero.

headline: The manager found the rest of the soundboard.
summary: Maintenance test alpha. Adds the latest recorded manager quips, makes quip preview playback stoppable, copies TTS scripts from the docs preview, and tightens diagnostics and AI image handling.

sections.title: What's new
items: Manager quip coverage now records shift starts, shift endings, early exits, bad fits, cool-downs, encouragement, brittle pairs, confirmed closures, and retention warnings so the standee has fewer silent clipboard moments.
The manager quip docs preview can copy TTS generation scripts and stop active playback from either the preview controls or the catalog row.
The manager quip catalog wraps long scripts and audio paths more cleanly so the review surface stops trying to win a width contest.

sections.title: Fixes and polish
items: Voice clip playback now returns a stop handle and tears down audio nodes when a preview is dismissed or replaced.
Settings diagnostics now build only when opened or copied, which keeps routine shell renders quieter while preserving the same exported payload.
Image attachments sent into date prompts are base64 encoded before reaching the model message, matching the provider contract after the AI SDK patch update.

headline: The release desk gets a stamp that means something.
summary: Maintenance test alpha. Promotes desktop release generation to the canon workflow, moves release checks before signing, and carries the player-facing changes from the unpublished v0.3.1 attempt.

sections.title: What's new
items: Release preflight now validates version identity, public notes, rendered GitHub notes, rendered updater notes, and a temporary updater manifest before the protected signing job asks for approval.
The release workflow doc now treats friend sharing as the canonical desktop release path, with a clear gate for versioned prerelease assets and the desktop-alpha updater channel.
The release notes regression test now follows the catalog dynamically so a version bump does not require editing a hard-coded expected list.

sections.title: Included from v0.3.1
items: Manager check-in popups now play in-game next to the standee at the named beats catalogued in the manager quips doc.
A new scenario joins the library: Dim Sum and Then Some, a brunch table that runs on lazy susan momentum.
Settings gains a sound effects panel so quip chimes, button clicks, and ambient cues can be tuned independently.
Tutorial coach marks now coordinate with the manager so a required step never has to compete with a voiced popup.

sections.title: Fixes and polish
items: The release workflow now records the v0.2.6 and v0.3.1 failure modes so future release debugging starts from the failed step instead of the nearest rumor.
Release note policy now explains how failed attempts affect the in-app What's new modal, GitHub release pages, and updater manifest notes.

headline: The manager stops waiting in the wings.
summary: Maintenance test alpha. Wires the voiced manager check-ins into the live shell, adds a sound effects panel, brings the scenario library to 56 dates, and tightens tutorial coordination.

sections.title: What's new
items: Manager check-in popups now play in-game next to the standee at the named beats catalogued in the manager quips doc, instead of living only in the docs preview.
A new scenario joins the library: Dim Sum and Then Some, a brunch table that runs on lazy susan momentum.
Settings gains a sound effects panel so quip chimes, button clicks, and ambient cues can be tuned independently.
Tutorial coach marks now coordinate with the manager so a required step never has to compete with a voiced popup.

sections.title: Fixes and polish
items: Renamed Temporal Coffee Shop to Cart Before The Horse, which describes the place more honestly.
Fixed a tutorial typing regression around list targets so coach mark spotlights resolve correctly when given an array of candidate elements.
Date engine and scenario card prompts received small cleanups for steadier AI behavior across the library.

headline: Orientation begins, and the manager finally has a face.
summary: Feature release. Ships an in-game tutorial, a voiced manager companion at named gameplay beats, scenario budgets that replace deck retirement, and pair-first booking in the Live Date room.

sections.title: What's new
items: First-time players are walked through the agency by lazy coach marks across onboarding, the dashboard, the date book, the roster, and pair memory, with a manager portrait companion replacing the old welcome modal.
Scenario booking now spends from a per-shift budget. Cards draw against pressure, intimacy, and chaos costs instead of being permanently retired, so the deck stays usable across the campaign.
Booking is pair-first inside the Live Date room: pick the focus case, pick the partner, then commit a date plan that fits the remaining budget.
A new manager check-in system reserves voiced sentences for named beats like date wraps, walkouts, brittle pair trajectories, retention warnings, and confirmed closures.
Settings adds a Reset orientation control so the tour can be replayed without wiping a save.

sections.title: Fixes and polish
items: Standee portrait cutouts ship with cleaner matte fringes after a sweep through the cutout pipeline.
Settings and dialog controls now share a form primitive library so inputs read the same across panels.
The roadmap workflow gained ready and review lanes so planned work has somewhere to sit between drafting and shipped.
The tutorial doc moved from roadmap to product, with a separate tutorial steps catalog for copy review.

sections.title: Save behavior
items: Save schema bumps from v7 to v8 to carry tutorial orientation state. Older alpha saves migrate forward on first load.
Players who already finished a tour see no replay unless they hit Reset orientation in Settings.

headline: The release courier found the right elevator.
summary: This maintenance test alpha retries the desktop release after fixing the Windows packaging workflow and the Python dependency graph manifest.

sections.title: Fixes
items: Desktop release packaging now renders updater and GitHub release notes with the runner Node runtime, avoiding the missing Vite Plus node subcommand in Actions.
The Tauri desktop build verifier uses the same direct Node command for local package builds.
Python portrait tooling now exposes a standard pip requirements manifest so GitHub's dependency graph job can read it.

sections.title: Included from v0.2.6
items: Docs routes load through the shared registry, and live-date autoplay keeps advancing after nonfatal AI retry notices.

headline: The release candidate survives its own inspection.
summary: This test alpha adds player-facing release notes, an active roadmap board, stronger pair memory follow-through, and the regression fixes found during a full AI date playthrough.

sections.title: Changelog
items: The app now shows a What's new modal for eligible saves after updates, with a Settings entry to reopen recent release notes.
Roadmap docs now include an active work board for temporary implementation plans and agent handoffs.
Pair memory, follow-up handling, and closure checks preserve more context for repeat bookings and case management.

sections.title: Fixes
items: Docs routes no longer crash when roadmap pages load through the shared docs registry.
Live-date autoplay keeps advancing after nonfatal AI retry notices instead of appearing to roll while no new beat is scheduled.
Release workflow, updater notes, and player install copy now use the checked-in release catalog for cleaner desktop handoffs.

headline: Pair memory starts doing the paperwork.
summary: Dates now leave more useful history behind, the roster and scenario pool are bigger, and the UI has better height discipline for the people standing in it.

sections.title: What's new
items: Pair memory now tracks agreements, open loops, and hidden relationship trajectory across repeat dates.
Eight new date scenarios join the library, including Cable Car Across Biomes, Hephaestus Forge, Long Afternoon Pool Bar, and Moonglass Kiln After Hours.
The roster grows to 40 members with Junie Marrow and Anubis added to the agency intake stack.
Product docs now live inside the app shell, so the field manual is closer to the machinery it keeps judging.

sections.title: Fixes and polish
items: Standee portraits now ground from visible feet, with character height canon documented for future lineup work.
AI date voice, output cleanup, and judge scoring received tighter guardrails.
Agent workflow instructions were updated so future changes have less room to invent policy with a clipboard.

headline: Release machinery stops narrating its own wrench drawer.
summary: This maintenance build fixed the GitHub release changelog range so new releases compare against the right prior version.

sections.title: Fixes
items: GitHub release changelog generation now finds the previous version tag more reliably.
Release notes stopped pulling the wrong history window when the desktop channel tag was nearby.

headline: Desktop packaging gets a second adult in the room.
summary: This maintenance build repaired the automated Windows packaging path used by the desktop release workflow.

sections.title: Fixes
items: The desktop release workflow now packages the signed Windows installer through the intended build path.
Release assets are uploaded with the installer, checksum, signature, player README, and updater manifest together.

headline: Preflight got stricter, which is rude but useful.
summary: This maintenance build fixed the automated release preflight so bad release candidates fail before signing starts.

sections.title: Fixes
items: The GitHub Action now runs the expected checks before requesting the signing environment.
Release validation blocks mismatched tags and package versions before the desktop build spends its afternoon.

headline: The lineup learned the difference between height and render height.
summary: Character height canon was split from source-scale render height so portraits can be judged without making every tall person a layout incident.

sections.title: What's new
items: Member fixtures now separate character height from standee render height.
The height lineup workflow was rebuilt for reviewing portrait scale consistently.

sections.title: Authoring notes
items: Future portrait work should fix source geometry or the shared source-scale registry, not one-off UI scale at call sites.

headline: The updater arrives, holding a signed clipboard.
summary: Feature release. Adds in-app desktop updates, expands the roster and scenario library, and moves the product docs into the app shell.

sections.title: What's new
items: Installed desktop builds can now check the desktop-alpha update channel and install signed updates from Settings.
The roster grows to 38 members, with five new profiles and a larger pair matrix.

The scenario library grows to 47 dates, adding Bank Heist 1920s Escape Room, Build-a-Bear Empty Mall, Cable Car Across Biomes, Colosseum Box Four, Empty Room Many Windows, Hephaestus Forge, Long Afternoon Pool Bar, and Moonglass Kiln After Hours.
Match-fit scoring was refactored for cleaner tag merging, clearer overlay rules, and stronger boundary-risk handling.
A top-level renderer error boundary now shows a recovery prompt instead of a blank screen.

sections.title: Fixes and polish
items: Settings now hosts updater controls alongside diagnostics and the dev-only member files toggle.
Splash screen roster count and starter stats match the larger roster.
Installer hooks and Tauri capabilities were tightened for updater-enabled desktop builds.

sections.title: Save behavior
items: No save schema change. Existing alpha saves should stay in place when updating.
Gateway keys stay in the desktop key file instead of the save file.
